name: Spanakopita
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large skillet over medium heat, heat oil and cook onions and green onions until tender and golden. Remove the skillet from the heat.
2. Drain the spinach and squeeze out any excess moisture. To the onion mixture in the skillet, add the spinach, cottage cheese, cream cheese, feta cheese, dill, pepper, and egg. Mix well.
3. Remove half of the phyllo sheets from the package; keep the remaining phyllo wrapped with plastic wrap to prevent drying out. Brush the bottom and sides of a 13x9-inch baking dish with some of the melted butter. Working quickly, place 2 sheets of phyllo in the dish, pressing them against the side. Lightly brush with butter. Repeat layering and brushing until the first half of the phyllo is used.
4. Preheat the oven to 350°F. Spread the spinach mixture evenly over the phyllo in the baking dish. Unwrap the remaining phyllo and place 2 sheets over the spinach mixture, allowing the edges to hang over the sides of the dish. Brush with butter, ensuring the edges are moistened. Repeat layering with the remaining phyllo and butter.
5. Gently roll the edges of the phyllo that are hanging over the pan inward to form an attractive border. Bake for 45 minutes or until the crust is golden and the filling is hot. Remove from the oven and let stand for 10 minutes before serving.
